task: I’ve got a situation at work where something is coming in a little out of spec, and there’s pressure to keep it moving because the schedule is tight. What’s the safest way to handle it and document it so I’m not guessing later?

turns.agent_response:
Safest approach: **don’t force a pass just to protect the schedule**. If it’s out of spec, borderline, or not fully verified, the safest call is to **hold it pending review** rather than accept it on pressure alone.

A simple decision rule:
- **Accept** only if it clearly meets spec and traceability is clean.
- **Hold** if it’s borderline, incomplete, or needs a second check.
- **Reject / write up** if it’s out of spec, repeated, or the lot looks suspect.

For documentation, keep it short and factual. A clean format could be:

**“Inspected X of Y. Most items matched documentation and spec. One item showed [issue] and was set aside pending recheck. Lot status held until verification is complete.”**

If you want, I can also help you turn this into a one-line note for your work system or a quick checklist you can use under time pressure.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s the right direction. But I’d want it tighter than that for a real shift. What do I put down so it’s defensible if somebody comes back later and asks why I held it up? I want a note that covers the measurement, the exact spec it missed, who I told, and what I did with the lot. Short, but solid.

turns.agent_response:
Use a note that captures four things: **what you checked, what missed, who you notified, and what you did next**.

A tight defensible format:

**“Checked [part/lot ID], [rev], [qty]. Measured [feature] at [value] vs spec [limit]. Result was out of spec / borderline. Notified [name/role] at [time]. Lot held / set aside pending review; no release until verification is complete.”**

If you want it even more shift-friendly, use this fill-in:

**“Inspected [X of Y]. [Part/lot/rev] measured [value] on [feature], spec [range]. Did not meet requirement. [Name/role] notified. Lot status held and segregated pending disposition.”**

A few guardrails to keep it solid:
- Put the **actual measured value**, not just “bad” or “off.”
- Quote the **exact spec or drawing limit**.
- Say **who you told** and **when** if you can.
- State the **physical action**: held, segregated, tagged, or returned.
- Don’t guess at disposition if it’s not clear yet.

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-line template** you can paste into your log every time.

user_simulator.text:
That works. Short enough to use on shift.

If I’m writing it for real, I’d want it like this:

**“Inspected [qty] of [part/lot]. [Feature] measured [value], below/above spec [limit]. [Name/role] notified at [time]. Lot held and segregated pending disposition; no release made.”**

That covers the measurement, the miss, who got told, and what happened to the lot. Clean. Defensible. No fluff.
